title: Terminal Mental Models, Help, and Evidence · Terminal Trace: Observation Before Change
summary: Investigate an unfamiliar Linux workstation without guessing, changing host state, or treating copied commands as explanations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux observation before change increment in a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change.
Use man pages, help, type, command -V, info, and project documentation to establish command authority and provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Observation Before Change
steps.instruction:

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Observation Before Change contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Observation Before Change while producing a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: On a worked example host, learner starts in /home/learner/lab with Bash 5.3, Coreutils 9.11, and exit status 1. Resolve ./data/../scripts, identify command authority, and separate terminal, shell, process, and kernel evidence. Competency probe: Capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change. A defensible solution applies Observation Before Change, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a terminal orientation and command-evidence notebook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-observation-before-change — Capture current user, directory, platform, environment, and relevant state before making a bounded change.
pwd
printf 'shell=%s\n' "$BASH_VERSION"
type -a printf
command -V pwd
printf 'status=%d\n' "$?"
content.caption: Runnable safe terminal evidence example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
